description.p: Flatpal is a calm, focused overview of the Flatpak apps on your system. It shows what is installed, what is running right now, and what is worth a look on Flathub — three tabs in one window.

The Running tab samples CPU and memory for every Flatpak sandbox live, with a per-sandbox breakdown when an app is running more than one. The Installed tab is a searchable list of your apps with summaries, versions, and install dates. The Explore tab is a browser for Flathub: a Popular shelf, full-text search across the catalog, an install-count chip on every row, and an Installed marker for apps you already have.

Whenever a remote has a newer version of one of your apps, an "Update available" pill surfaces on the matching row in every tab; the detail page grows a tinted callout with the version diff and inline release notes for everything between the installed and available versions. Flatpal itself only surfaces the update — handing it off to GNOME Software keeps the actual install / uninstall flow somewhere the user already trusts.
Flatpal does not replace flatpak, GNOME Software, or any other tool — it sits next to them and makes it easier to see your Flatpaks at a glance.

screenshot.caption: The Running tab with CPU and memory sampled live for each sandbox
The Installed tab listing every Flatpak with summary and version
The Explore tab browsing Flathub by popularity
A detail page for an installed app, with the Open app action
A detail page showing the app's own screenshots and About metadata

ul.li: Screenshot cache now persists between sessions when running as a Flatpak, so detail pages no longer re-download every screenshot on each visit.
Startup cache pruning now operates on the correct directory, keeping the screenshot cache from growing unbounded.
First public release of Flatpal — a calm GTK4/libadwaita overview of your installed, running, and Flathub Flatpaks, with three tabs: Running, Installed, and Explore.
Detail page for any app showing screenshots, description, sandbox permissions, and a one-click **Open app** action.
Running tab now expands each app into per-sandbox sub-rows showing the command line, with live CPU and memory updates.
Freeze-position toggle and refresh-interval picker on the Running tab to keep rows steady while you read them.
Clicking the "sorted by …" pill on any tab opens the sort menu, anchored to the header button.
Explore tab marks already-installed apps with a mint-teal **Installed** pill.
Running tab opens with a clear "Looking for running Flatpaks…" loading state instead of a blank "Nothing running" message.
Installed tab now shows install dates for apps in non-English locales.
Host-installed Flatpak icons (e.g. Black Box) now appear correctly in the Running and Installed tabs.
Running tab sampling is roughly 5× faster after the first sample.
Tab labels and row layouts hold steady as content loads — no more width or height shifts between samples.
Long app titles are truncated cleanly on a single line.
